title: Browser Presigning, POST Policy, and CORS · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for browser presigning, post policy, and cors and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege browser upload session. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Use @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner 3.1087.0 getSignedUrl with an exact PutObjectCommand region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption choice, and required browser-header parity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Direct Upload Finalization
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3434 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to finalize a browser upload intent while separating CORS from IAM and promise completion; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, finalize by matching intent to HEAD or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Direct Upload Finalization.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, finalize by matching intent to head or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Direct Upload Finalization, keep Direct Upload Finalization connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
